raw:
**SCENE ANALYSIS - A white faucet is mounted on a wall above a sink. The faucet has a handle on its left side. The task is to turn the left tap. STRATEGY - First, reach and grasp the left tap handle. Then, rotate the handle to turn the tap. Failure modes include misgrasping the handle or insufficient rotation. AFFORDANCE REASONING - Step 1 reach the faucet body to approach the handle; Step 2 grasp the left tap handle for control; Step 3 rotate the handle to turn the tap.**
Goal: turned(left_tap)
Scene: faucet, sink, wall
Step 1: reach(faucet)
  affordance_region: the body of the white faucet
  contact: gripper_state(open) [progress]
  spatial: distance(gripper, faucet, <, 0.03) [completion]
  direction: approach_axis(0, 0, -1) [progress]
  done_when: distance(gripper, faucet) < 0.03
Step 2: grasp(left_tap)
  affordance_region: the left tap handle of the faucet
  contact: holding(left_tap) [completion]
  direction: grasp_axis(0, 0, -1) [progress]
  done_when: holding(left_tap)
Step 3: rotate(left_tap)
  affordance_region: the left tap handle of the faucet
  contact: holding(left_tap) [progress]
  direction: rotate_axis(0, 0, 90) [progress]
  done_when: turned(left_tap)
<END_OF_PLAN>

steps.affordance_region: the body of the white faucet
the left tap handle of the faucet
the left tap handle of the faucet
